questions.question: Which among the following form of painting is basically a religious art?
questions.explanation: Thangka is a traditional Tibetan form of religious art originating in India. It is related to Buddhism. They are scroll paintings usually framed in colorful silk brocade and have a thin silk veil covering the front surface. They are highly religious, and possess a unique art style of their own.

questions.question: Which among the following traditional dance belongs to state of Jammu & Kashmir?
questions.explanation: Rauf is traditional dance of J&K. It is performed by a group of women facing each other lined up in two lines and dressed in beautiful costumes. The dance has simple footwork which also called as Chakri in local language. The dance is often performed in spring season to celebrate good weather.

questions.question: Kalaripayattu is a martial art form of which of the following state?
questions.explanation: Kalarippayattu is the indigenous martial art of Kerala. Kalaripayattu techniques are a combination of steps (Chuvatu) and postures (Vadivu). It has reference in Tamil Sangam Literature. Each stance has its own style, power combination, function and effectiveness.

questions.question: Ambubhachi Festival is associated with which state?
questions.explanation: It is a festival of Kamakhya temple, Guwhati, Assam. It is celebrated every year during monsoon season and ambubachi mela is also held. The Kamakhya Temple is known to be one of the 52 shakti peethas of goddess Shakti. It is associated with tantric cult and this festival is also known as "mahakumbh of east".

questions.question: Which among the following festival is associated with agricultural practice Jhumming?
questions.explanation: Chapchar Kut festival held during the period when the bamboos and trees that have been cut down are being awaited to dry to be burnt for jhumming. It marked the end of the Jhum clearing, readying the field for sowing and the festive spirit lasts from three to seven days in March. The traditional costume parades, showcasing dances like cheraw, chai, chheihlam, sarlamkai and musical performances by groups, among others, are part of the celebrations.

questions.question: Which among the art form is related to mimicry?
questions.explanation: The art of the bahurupiya is the ability to create convincing impersonations of identifiable types. It is associated with Bhands, but formerly members of various castes, including Brahmans, practiced this art in villages as well as courts.

questions.question: Lad Khan Temple is situated in which of the following state?
questions.explanation: The Lad Khan Temple, dedicated to Shiva, is located in Aihole in the state of Karnataka. The temple was built in a Panchayatana style. The temple is named after a person named Lad khan, who turned this temple into his residence for a short period.

questions.question: Which among the following temple is NOT a part of UNESCO World Heritage Site?
questions.explanation: Tungnath is the highest Shiva temple in the world and is the highest of the five Panch Kedar temples, in Uttrakhand. It is linked to the origin of the Panch Kedar temples built by the Pandavas. It is made of black rock, and the construction style is similar to the temple in Kedarnath.

questions.question: Teppotsavam is celebrated in which of the following state?
questions.explanation: It is a 5-day annual float festival held in Telangana celebrated in month of Chaitra. In this festival, a Hamsavahanam' (swan-shaped boat), carried the processional idols of Lord Rama and His consort Sita in Godavari river.

questions.question: Jallikattu event is a part of which of the following festival?
questions.explanation: Jallikattu is a bull-vaulting event practised in Tamil Nadu as a part of Pongal celebrations on Mattu Pongal day. Participants tackle the bull in an arena by its hump and try to hang on till they cross the finishing line. It is an ancient sport having references in Sangam literature. It is also called as Eru Thazhuvuthal (hugging the bull).

questions.question: "Joseph Anton" is the autobiography of who among the following?
questions.explanation: Joseph Anton: A Memoir is an autobiographical book by Salman Rushdie. The memoir is unusual in the sense that Rushdie writes about his life as 'Joseph Anton' in the third person rather than the first person. He chose the name to honor the writers Joseph Conrad and Anton Chekhov.

questions.question: The movie Slumdog Millionaire was based on which among the following book?
questions.explanation: The movie was based on the book Q&A. It was written by Vikas Swarup. Q & A won the South Africa's Boeke Prize 2006. It was also nominated for the Best First Book by the Commonwealth Writers' Prize and won the Prix Grand Public at the 2007 Paris Book Fair.

questions.question: "Indian Parliamentary Diplomacy" was written by?
questions.explanation: The book "Indian Parliamentary Diplomacy" written by Smt. Meira Kumar, former Speaker, Lok Sabha. The book is an account of the role of parliamentary diplomacy in furthering foreign policy and strengthening international relations. The volume is a collection of reports and select speeches that Smt. Kumar as Lok Sabha Speaker and leader of Indian Parliamentary Delegations delivered at international conferences and during her visits abroad.

questions.question: The book "Fragile Frontiers" deals with which of the following?
questions.explanation: Fragile Frontiers: The Secret History of Mumbai Terror Attacks is a book by Indian writer Saroj Kumar Rath. The book is about terrorism in South Asia, centering on the 2008 Mumbai attacks.

questions.question: The book "The Crisis Within" deals with which of the following?
questions.explanation: The book was authored by Ganesh Devy. It deals with Knowledge and Education in India. It points out the lack of infrastructure, adequate funding and genuine autonomy within educational institutions, departments within those institutions and individuals who teach in those departments.

questions.question: Who is the author of the book "The people's President: Dr. A.P.J.Abdul Kalam"?
questions.explanation: It was authored by S.M.Khan. The book gives a comprehensive compendium of Dr Kalam's experiences as the President of India and his post-presidency days.

questions.question: Who among the following current/former Chief Minister of Indian State is the author of the book "My Unforgettable Memories"?
questions.explanation: West Bengal Chief Minster Mamatha Banerjee is the author of the book "My Unforgettable Memories". It gives an account of her life, right from her youth to her political career.

questions.question: "My Favourite Nature Stories" was authored by?
questions.explanation: The book was written by Ruskin Bond. It talks about his various encounters with the natural world. It also gives a picture of his life in Mussoorie, India.

questions.question: Which among the following is the final part of JK Rowling's Harry Potter Series?
options: Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince
Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone
Harry Potter and the Cursed Child
Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban
Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows
questions.explanation: Harry Potter is a series of fantasy novels written by British author J. K. Rowling. The first novel, Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone and last one of the series was Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows.

questions.question: Apart from JK Rowling, who among the following has co-authored the new story "Harry Potter and the Cursed Child"?
questions.explanation: Harry Potter and the Cursed Child is a two-part stage play written by Jack Thorne based on an original new story by Thorne, J. K. Rowling and John Tiffany. The story begins nineteen years after the events of Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows.

questions.question: Which among the following sporting awards are given for lifetime achievements?
questions.explanation: The Dhyan Chand Award is the lifetime achievement sporting honour presented in India. It is also known as Dhyan Chand Award for Lifetime Achievement in Sports and Games. It is awarded annually by the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports. The award is given only to the disciplines included in the events like Olympic Games, Paralympic Games, Asian Games, Commonwealth Games, World Championship and World Cup along with Cricket, Indigenous Games, and Parasports.

questions.question: Dhronacharya award was first instituted in which year?
questions.explanation: Dhronacharya award was first instituted in the year 1985. It is awarded to honor sport coaches in India. The award is given only to the disciplines included in the events like Olympic Games, Paralympic Games, Asian Games, Commonwealth Games, World Championship and World Cup along with Cricket, Indigenous Games, and Parasports. The first recipients of the award were Bhalachandra Bhaskar Bhagwat (Wrestling), Om Prakash Bhardwaj (Boxing), and O. M. Nambiar (Athletics).

questions.question: Who among the following is the first woman recipient of Fields Medal?
questions.explanation: Maryam Mirzakhani, an Iranian mathematician who was the only woman ever to win a Fields Medal. She has also won the gold medal in the International Mathematical Olympiad. Fields Medal is associated with Mathematics. It is the highest scientific award for mathematicians, and is presented every four years at the International Congress of Mathematicians. The awards recognize both existing work and the promise of future achievement, presented to mathematicians not over forty at the year of the Congress.

questions.question: DSC Prize is associated with which field?
questions.explanation: The DSC Prize for South Asian Literature is an international literary prize awarded annually to writers of any ethnicity or nationality writing about South Asia themes such as culture, politics, history, or people. It is for an original full-length novel written in English, or translated into English.

questions.question: DSC Prize was first awarded in the year?
questions.explanation: The DSC Prize was instituted by Surina Narula and Manhad Narula in 2010 and was first awarded in the year 2011. It was presented to H.M.Naqvi for his work Home Boy. The winner receives 25,000 USD.

questions.question: Who is the first Indian to receive DSC Prize?
questions.explanation: Jeet Thayil is an Indian poet, novelist, librettist and musician. He was the first Indian to receive the award for his work Narcopolis. The DSC Prize for South Asian Literature is an international literary prize awarded annually to writers of any ethnicity or nationality writing about South Asia themes such as culture, politics, history, or people.

questions.question: Which among the following awards are instituted by Ministry of Labour & Employment?
questions.explanation: All the above-mentioned awards along with National Safety Awards (Factories) are also instituted by Ministry of Labour & Employment. The objective of the Prime Ministers Shram Awards is to recognize the outstanding contributions made by workmen as defined in the Industrial Dispute Act, 1947 in organizations both in public and private sector and who have distinguished record of performance, devotion to duty of a high order, specific contribution in the field of productivity, proven innovative abilities, presence of mind and exceptional courage and also to the workmen who have made supreme sacrifice of laying down their lives in the conscientious discharge of their duties. Vishwakarma Rashtriya Purashkar is for rewarding & recognizing the efforts of workers from the industrial undertakings at the national level. National Safety Awards to promote a competitive spirit amongst mine/factory operators for the betterment of safety standards in mines/factories and to give due recognition to outstanding safety performance at national level.

questions.question: Medini Purashkar Award is presented by which among the following ministry?
options: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
Ministry of Women and Child Development
Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
questions.explanation: It is presented by Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change. This award is given to Indian authors each year to encourage original works in Hindi on environment and its related subjects such as wildlife, water resources and conservation. Four cash awards are presented under this category.

questions.question: National Photography Award is instituted by which ministry?
options: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
questions.explanation: The award is instituted by Ministry of information and Broadcasting. The objective is to promote Nation's, Art, Culture, Development, Heritage, History, Life, People, Society, Traditions, etc with art and technique of photography and to encourage professional and amateur photographers from all corners of the country.

questions.question: The book "Fearless in Opposition: Power and Accountability" was authored by?
questions.explanation: It was written by P. Chidambaram, former Finance Minister. It is a collection of the finest and most incisive essays published as a weekly column in the Indian EXPRESS by the author. He answers questions to some contentious issues under the rule of NDA government.
